text:
Hot take:

Designers who can't code will be more valuable than designers who code in 2 years.

Why? AI writes code now.

But AI can't see what you envision.

Your taste becomes the bottleneck, not your technical skills.

Building tools for this future.
linkText:
That's why I built jsOM - your design becomes THE spec, not a reference.

https://github.com/AleMusica/jsom

⭐ if you believe design > code

text:
Unpopular opinion:

Figma is becoming irrelevant.

Not because it's bad - it's excellent.

But it exports pixels.
AI needs structure.

The gap between "what you designed" and "what AI builds" is growing.

Someone needs to bridge it.
linkText:
I'm building that bridge.

jsOM: draw UI → export LLM-readable spec → AI builds exactly what you designed.

https://github.com/AleMusica/jsom ⭐

text:
I spent 3 days building something no one asked for.

Zero stars. Zero sponsors.

But every time I use Claude to build UI, I think:

"Why am I describing what I can see in my head?"

So I built a way to SHOW it instead.

Open source. MIT. Yours.
linkText:
jsOM - the design tool that speaks LLM

https://github.com/AleMusica/jsom

⭐ if you've felt this frustration

text:
Genuine question for designers:

How do you communicate with AI coding agents?

a) Screenshots
b) Text descriptions
c) Figma links
d) Something else?

I built a tool based on option (d).

Curious what you're using.
linkText:
Option d = draw directly, export structured spec LLMs understand.

https://github.com/AleMusica/jsom

Would love your feedback.

text:
The AI coding workflow is broken.

Here's what I mean 🧵
thread:
1/ You have an idea. Crystal clear in your head.

You open Claude/GPT. Start typing.

"Make a dashboard with..." and suddenly you're writing 500 words describing something you could draw in 30 seconds.

2/ Or worse - you screenshot Figma.

AI sees pixels. Guesses structure. Hallucinates components.

You spend more time fixing than building.

3/ The problem isn't AI. AI is brilliant.

The problem is INPUT.

We're giving AI the wrong format.

4/ What if your design WAS the spec?

Not a reference. Not "inspiration."

THE actual specification AI executes.

5/ That's what I built.

jsOM: infinite canvas → LLM-readable export → any AI builds exactly what you designed.

Open source. Works offline with Ollama.

https://github.com/AleMusica/jsom

⭐ if this resonates

text:
POV: You're a solo founder

Design tool: $15/mo
AI coding: $20/mo
Hosting: $20/mo
Domain: $12/yr

...still copying screenshots into ChatGPT and praying it understands your layout.

There has to be a better way.
linkText:
Built the better way.

Free. Open source. Works offline.

https://github.com/AleMusica/jsom ⭐

text:
Respect to @v0 for prompt → code.

But sometimes I know exactly what I want.

I don't want AI to interpret my words.
I want AI to execute my vision.

Different problem. Different tool.

Complement, not compete.
linkText:
jsOM handles the step before:

visual design → structured spec → v0/Claude/any AI

https://github.com/AleMusica/jsom
